summary: I was born in Burma, a beautiful place surrounded by forests, rivers, and wildlife. We lived by hunting and farming, enjoying the sunshine and natural views. However, Burma was dangerous because of the civil war between the Karen and Burmese people. When I was just over a year old, my village was attacked by Burmese soldiers who destroyed our homes and farms. Many of us had to flee for our lives to escape death or capture.

After fleeing the violence in Burma, my family and I sought safety in Thailand. We were helped by Thai soldiers who guided us through the forest to a safer place. Thailand became a refuge for us after the brutal attacks and hardships we faced in Burma. We finally arrived at a place called Mae Kha Khe in Thailand, where we could live without fear of Burmese soldiers.

My family is part of the Karen people, who have been caught in the middle of the civil war in Burma. We had to run together through the forest to survive the attacks by Burmese soldiers. The Karen community looked out for each other during these dangerous times, helping one another escape and survive. Many Karen were captured and forced to work for the Burmese soldiers, facing torture and death if they resisted.

Mae Kha Khe is the place in Thailand where my family finally found safety after fleeing Burma. It was a relief to arrive there after running barefoot through rough forest terrain. Thanks to the help of Thai soldiers, we reached Mae Kha Khe, which became our new home away from the dangers of the civil war. It represents a turning point from fear and violence to hope and security for us.
